title_id: Kekayaan Naratif dan Kisah-Kisah Para Nabi
content_id:
Salah satu fitur paling khas dari Tafsir al-Khazin adalah konten naratifnya yang luas — kisah-kisah saleh, akun-akun nabawi, dan elaborasi isyarat-isyarat al-Quran yang membuat karya ini menarik bagi pembaca yang luas. Al-Khazin mengikuti tradisi menyertakan Isra'iliyyat di samping materi Islam yang ditransmisikan, memperkaya komentarnya dengan detail-detail naratif yang al-Quran sendiri hanya menyebutnya secara singkat.

Untuk kisah-kisah al-Quran tentang para nabi, al-Khazin menyediakan pengembangan naratif yang lebih luas daripada yang biasanya dikandung oleh karya-karya tafsir yang ketat secara ilmiah. Kisah Yusuf dalam Surah Yusuf, misalnya, mendapat elaborasi naratif yang luas mengambil dari tradisi Islam qisas al-anbiya (kisah-kisah para nabi), yang menggabungkan materi dari sumber-sumber biblika dan para-biblika yang ditransformasi melalui lensa interpretatif Islam. Perlakuan al-Khazin membuat kisah tersebut terasa hidup dan langsung secara emosional bagi pembaca.

Similarly, akun-akunnya tentang para nabi Ibrahim, Musa, dan Isa mengambil dari tradisi-tradisi naratif yang memperluas referensi-referensi al-Quran menjadi kisah-kisah yang lebih lengkap. Perluasan-perluasan ini mencakup detail-detail — sifat pasti perdebatan Ibrahim dengan ayahnya, kata-kata khusus pertukaran Musa dengan Firaun, mukjizat-mukjizat yang terkait dengan pelayanan Isa — yang berasal dari Isra'iliyyat daripada hadis yang terauthentikasi. Al-Khazin menyajikan detail-detail ini sebagai bagian dari tradisi interpretatif tanpa secara konsisten menandai status evidentalnya.

Untuk bagian-bagian tentang keutamaan surah-surah al-Quran (fada'il as-suwar), al-Khazin menyertakan riwayat-riwayat yang diidentifikasi oleh kritikus-kritikus hadis kemudian sebagai lemah atau palsu. Riwayat-riwayat ini — yang mengaitkan pahala-pahala spesifik untuk melafalkan surah-surah tertentu — beredar luas dalam tradisi Islam populer dan masuk ke dalam banyak teks-teks devotional. Kehadirannya dalam al-Khazin berkontribusi pada popularitas karya tersebut sambil juga menjadi titik fokus evaluasi kritis hadis kemudian.

Terlepas dari kekhawatiran-kekhawatiran ini, kekayaan naratif Tafsir al-Khazin mempertahankan gambaran budaya devotional dan sastra kesalehan Islam abad pertengahan yang tidak ditangkap oleh karya-karya tafsir yang murni teknis.
content_en:
One of the most distinctive features of Tafsir al-Khazin is its extensive narrative content — the pious stories, prophetic accounts, and elaborations of Quranic allusions that made the work engaging for a wide readership. Al-Khazin followed the tradition of including Isra'iliyyat alongside transmitted Islamic material, enriching his commentary with narrative details that the Quran itself only briefly references.

For the Quranic stories of the prophets, al-Khazin provided more extensive narrative development than strictly scholarly tafsir works typically contain. The story of Yusuf in Surah Yusuf, for instance, receives extensive narrative elaboration drawing on the Islamic tradition of qisas al-anbiya (prophetic stories), which incorporated material from biblical and para-biblical sources transformed through an Islamic interpretive lens. Al-Khazin's treatment makes the story vivid and emotionally immediate for the reader.

Similarly, his accounts of the prophets Ibrahim, Musa, and Isa draw on narrative traditions that expand the Quranic references into fuller stories. These expansions include details — the precise nature of Ibrahim's debate with his father, the specific words of Musa's exchanges with Pharaoh, the miracles associated with Isa's ministry — that derive from Isra'iliyyat rather than authenticated hadith. Al-Khazin presents these details as part of the interpretive tradition without consistently flagging their evidentiary status.

For the sections on the virtues of Quranic surahs (fada'il as-suwar), al-Khazin includes narrations that later hadith critics identified as weak or fabricated. These narrations — attributing specific rewards for reciting specific surahs — circulated widely in the popular Islamic tradition and found their way into many devotional texts. Their presence in al-Khazin contributed to the work's popularity while also becoming a focal point of later hadith-critical evaluation.

Despite these concerns, the narrative richness of Tafsir al-Khazin preserves a picture of the devotional and literary culture of medieval Islamic piety that purely technical tafsir works do not capture.
content_ar: One of the most distinctive features of التفسير al-Khazin is its extensive narrative content — the pious stories, prophetic accounts, and elaborations of القرآني allusions that made the work engaging for a wide readership. Al-Khazin followed the التراث of بما في ذلك Isra'iliyyat alongside transmitted Islamic material, enriching his commentary with narrative details that the القرآن الكريم itself only briefly references. For the القرآني stories of the الأنبياء, al-Khazin provided more extensive narrative development than strictly العلمي التفسير works typically contain. The story of يوسف ﷺ in السورة يوسف ﷺ, على سبيل المثال, receives extensive narrative elaboration drawing on the Islamic التراث of qisas al-anbiya (prophetic stories), which incorporated material from biblical and para-biblical sources transformed through an Islamic interpretive lens. Al-Khazin's treatment makes the story vivid and emotionally immediate for the reader. Similarly, his accounts of the الأنبياء إبراهيم ﷺ, موسى ﷺ, and عيسى draw on narrative traditions that expand the القرآني references into fuller stories. These expansions include details — the precise nature of إبراهيم ﷺ's debate with his الأب, the specific words of موسى ﷺ's exchanges with Pharaoh, the miracles associated with عيسى's ministry — that derive from Isra'iliyyat rather than authenticated الحديث. Al-Khazin presents these details as part of the interpretive التراث without consistently flagging their evidentiary status. For the sections on the الفضائل of القرآني السور (fada'il as-suwar), al-Khazin includes narrations that later الحديث critics identified as weak or fabricated. These narrations — attributing specific rewards for reciting specific السور — circulated widely in the popular Islamic التراث and found their الطريق into many devotional texts. Their presence in al-Khazin contributed to the work's popularity بينما أيضاً becoming a focal point of later الحديث-البالغ الأهمية evaluation. رغم these concerns, the narrative richness of التفسير al-Khazin preserves a picture of the devotional and literary culture of medieval Islamic piety that purely technical التفسير works do not capture.
